prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetheart, a CPU, or Central Processing Unit, is like the brain of the computer. It helps the computer think and make decisions, just like how you use your brain to solve puzzles or figure things out. When you play games or do your homework on the computer, the CPU is working hard to make everything happen smoothly. Think of it as a busy little worker that takes all the information and makes sense of it for you. Just like how I might help you bake cookies by making sure we have all the right ingredients, the CPU helps gather and organize all the bits of information your computer needs!
